---
applyTo: "skillexa-portal/**"
---

# Internationalization (i18n) — Instructions

## Golden Rule

**No hardcoded user-facing text anywhere in the codebase.** Every string shown to the user — labels, buttons, headings, placeholders, error messages, tooltips, ARIA attributes, notifications, confirmation dialogs — must come from a translation message file.

## Supported Locales

| Code | Language | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `en` | English | **Default** / fallback |
| `ru` | Russian | |
| `de` | German | |

## Library

Use **`next-intl`** — the de-facto i18n solution for the Next.js App Router with first-class Server Component support.

```
pnpm add next-intl
```

## File Structure

```
skillexa-portal/
  i18n/
    request.ts              # next-intl request config (reads locale, loads messages)
    routing.ts              # locale routing config (locales, defaultLocale, pathnames)
  messages/
    en.json                 # English messages (source of truth)
    ru.json                 # Russian messages (must mirror en.json keys exactly)
    de.json                 # German messages (must mirror en.json keys exactly)
  app/
    [locale]/               # dynamic locale segment (replaces old app/ root)
      layout.tsx            # root layout with NextIntlClientProvider
      page.tsx
      (auth)/
      (dashboard)/
      ...
  middleware.ts             # next-intl middleware for locale detection/redirect
  next.config.ts            # next-intl plugin integration
```

## Message File Format

Organize messages into **namespaces** matching feature areas. Use nested JSON objects:

```jsonc
// messages/en.json
{
  "common": {
    "appName": "Skillexa",
    "loading": "Loading…",
    "save": "Save",
    "cancel": "Cancel",
    "delete": "Delete",
    "confirm": "Confirm",
    "error": "Something went wrong",
    "retry": "Retry"
  },
  "auth": {
    "login": "Log in",
    "logout": "Log out",
    "email": "Email",
    "password": "Password",
    "loginButton": "Sign in",
    "invalidCredentials": "Invalid email or password"
  },
  "jobs": {
    "title": "My Jobs",
    "status": {
      "pending": "Pending",
      "processing": "Processing",
      "succeeded": "Succeeded",
      "failed": "Failed"
    },
    "download": "Download PDF",
    "noJobs": "You have no jobs yet"
  },
  "documents": {
    "title": "Create Document",
    "submit": "Generate"
  },
  "validation": {
    "required": "{field} is required",
    "minLength": "{field} must be at least {min} characters",
    "email": "Enter a valid email address"
  }
}
```

### Message Rules

- **`en.json` is the source of truth.** Add keys here first, then add corresponding translations to `ru.json` and `de.json`.
- All three files must have **identical key structures** at all times.
- Use **ICU-style interpolation** for dynamic values: `"greeting": "Hello, {name}!"`.
- Use **rich text** markup when needed: `"terms": "By signing up you agree to our <link>Terms</link>"`.
- Use **plural rules** via ICU: `"itemCount": "You have {count, plural, =0 {no items} one {# item} other {# items}}"`.
- Namespace naming follows feature folders (e.g., `auth`, `jobs`, `documents`, `common`, `validation`).

## Configuration

### `i18n/routing.ts`

```ts
import { defineRouting } from 'next-intl/routing';

export const routing = defineRouting({
  locales: ['en', 'ru', 'de'],
  defaultLocale: 'en',
});
```

### `i18n/request.ts`

```ts
import { getRequestConfig } from 'next-intl/server';
import { routing } from './routing';

export default getRequestConfig(async ({ requestLocale }) => {
  let locale = await requestLocale;

  if (!locale || !routing.locales.includes(locale as any)) {
    locale = routing.defaultLocale;
  }

  return {
    locale,
    messages: (await import(`../messages/${locale}.json`)).default,
  };
});
```

### `middleware.ts`

```ts
import createMiddleware from 'next-intl/middleware';
import { routing } from './i18n/routing';

export default createMiddleware(routing);

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/((?!api|_next|.*\\..*).*)'],
};
```

### `next.config.ts`

```ts
import createNextIntlPlugin from 'next-intl/plugin';
import type { NextConfig } from 'next';

const withNextIntl = createNextIntlPlugin('./i18n/request.ts');

const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
  output: 'standalone',
};

export default withNextIntl(nextConfig);
```

## Usage Patterns

### Server Components (default)

```tsx
import { useTranslations } from 'next-intl';

export default function JobsPage() {
  const t = useTranslations('jobs');

  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{t('title')}</h1>
      <p>{t('noJobs')}</p>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Client Components

```tsx
'use client';

import { useTranslations } from 'next-intl';

export function LoginForm() {
  const t = useTranslations('auth');

  return (
    <Button type="submit">{t('loginButton')}</Button>
  );
}
```

### Dynamic Values

```tsx
const t = useTranslations('validation');
// messages: { "required": "{field} is required" }
t('required', { field: t('auth.email') });
```

### Metadata / `generateMetadata`

```tsx
import { getTranslations } from 'next-intl/server';

export async function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: Promise<{ locale: string }> }) {
  const { locale } = await params;
  const t = await getTranslations({ locale, namespace: 'jobs' });
  return { title: t('title') };
}
```

### Navigation Links

Use `next-intl/navigation` helpers to generate locale-aware links:

```tsx
import { Link } from '@/i18n/navigation';

<Link href="/jobs">…</Link>
```

Create `i18n/navigation.ts`:

```ts
import { createNavigation } from 'next-intl/navigation';
import { routing } from './routing';

export const { Link, redirect, usePathname, useRouter } = createNavigation(routing);
```

## Locale Layout

The root layout under `app/[locale]/layout.tsx` must:

1. Receive `locale` from params.
2. Set `<html lang={locale}>`.
3. Wrap children with `NextIntlClientProvider`.

```tsx
import { NextIntlClientProvider } from 'next-intl';
import { getMessages } from 'next-intl/server';

export default async function LocaleLayout({
  children,
  params,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  params: Promise<{ locale: string }>;
}) {
  const { locale } = await params;
  const messages = await getMessages();

  return (
    <html lang={locale}>
      <body>
        <NextIntlClientProvider locale={locale} messages={messages}>
          {children}
        </NextIntlClientProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

## Mantine Integration

When using Mantine components, always pass translated strings — never raw text:

```tsx
const t = useTranslations('auth');

<TextInput label={t('email')} placeholder={t('emailPlaceholder')} />
<Button>{t('loginButton')}</Button>
<Notification title={t('common.error')} />
```

For Mantine form validation messages, pass translated strings from `useTranslations('validation')`.

## Adding a New Locale

1. Add the locale code to the `locales` array in `i18n/routing.ts`.
2. Create `messages/{locale}.json` mirroring the full key structure of `en.json`.
3. No code changes needed — middleware and request config pick it up automatically.

## Code Review Checklist

- [ ] No hardcoded user-facing strings in `.tsx` / `.ts` files.
- [ ] New keys added to **all three** message files (`en.json`, `ru.json`, `de.json`).
- [ ] Key structures are identical across all message files.
- [ ] Dynamic values use ICU interpolation syntax (`{variable}`), not string concatenation.
- [ ] `useTranslations` (client/server) or `getTranslations` (async server) used correctly.
- [ ] Page metadata (`title`, `description`) is also translated via `generateMetadata`.
- [ ] ARIA labels and accessibility text are translated.
